snps.effect_template: 9p21.3 locus — most replicated CAD risk locus. G allele increases risk ~1.3x per copy.
9p21.3 variant independently associated with CAD and MI risk.
Lipoprotein(a) risk variant. G allele increases CAD risk through lipoprotein(a) elevation.
SORT1 sortilin protective variant. G allele protective against CAD (OR ~0.87 per copy).
PCSK9 loss-of-function protective variant. T allele significantly reduces LDL-C and CAD risk.
LDLR LDL receptor protective variant. T allele protective against CAD.
PHACTR1 phosphatase risk variant. C allele associated with increased CAD susceptibility.
APOB apolipoprotein B risk variant. T allele increases CAD risk through LDL metabolism.

recommendation_template: Monitor standard cardiovascular risk factors (blood pressure, cholesterol, blood sugar). Lifestyle modifications have strong evidence for risk reduction.

studies.title: A common allele on chromosome 9 associated with coronary heart disease
studies.finding: 9p21.3 locus associated with ~30% increased risk per allele for CAD

studies.title: Large-scale association analysis identifies 13 new susceptibility loci for coronary artery disease
studies.finding: Large-scale GWAS identifying 13 novel CAD susceptibility loci including LPA, PCSK9, SORT1, and others
